_mission: Train HOW students speak, not WHAT they know.
_core_rule: Text is always on screen. Student never generates content. Only job is delivery.

_waveform_rule.why: Students learn one visual rule: green = target, blue = me. Never swap.

meta.lesson_id: string — unique across all lessons, e.g. lesson_001
meta.lesson_type: scrambled_story | pace_difference | emotion_difference | character_read | tongue_twister | speed_reader | listen_and_echo | scene_speak | comic_reader | refresher | topic_read | delivery_flashcard
meta.title: string — display name shown on screen
meta.subtitle: string — one-line description shown under title
meta.skill: string — single skill trained, e.g. Vocal Variety and Delivery
signals: pronunciation | prosody | speaking_rate | fluency | volume | stress | task_adherence

rounds._note: Every lesson has 1–3 rounds. Every round has content + model + practice + perform. Exception: refresher has no model or practice — see lesson_types.
rounds.instruction: string — shown above the round on screen

content._note: Shape varies by lesson type. See content_schemas below for each type.

model._note: App demonstrates how it should sound. Student watches and listens only. Zero pressure.
model.label: string — e.g. 👀 Watch First | 🎧 Listen First | 👀 Hear All 3
model.prompt_text: string — instruction shown above the demo

highlight_words: array of key words to highlight
display.plays_sequence: boolean — true when model plays multiple versions

plays_sequence: array of sequence items when multiple versions played — e.g. emotions, speeds, characters

visual_cue.expression: string — e.g. confident | excited | storytelling | focused
visual_cue.animation: string — e.g. talking | jumping | slow_nod | speed_dial_sweeping

model.advance_label: string — e.g. Got it! Let me try →

practice._note: Student imitates once. NOT scored. Hints visible. Can skip. Mistakes cost nothing.
practice.label: string — e.g. 🗣️ Practice Try
practice.prompt_text: string — low pressure, never mentions scoring

display.placeholder_text: string — shown when sentence is hidden
display.hint_text: string — specific delivery tip for this lesson type

reveal_button.label: string — e.g. 👁️ Peek at sentence
reveal_button.reveal_for_seconds: integer — how long sentence shows before hiding again

messages: array of positive strings — no numbers, no scores ever

practice.advance_label: string — e.g. Ready for the real thing! →

perform._note: Student records for real. SCORED. Hints hidden. Waveform comparison shown after.
perform.label: string — e.g. 🎯 Real Attempt

display.show_one_at_a_time: boolean — for multi-version lessons (emotions, characters, speeds)

recording.record_each_separately: boolean — true for emotion, character, speed, tongue_twister
recording.max_seconds_per_item: integer — used when record_each_separately is true

scoring.score_each_separately: boolean — true for emotion, character, speed
scoring.comparison_label: string — e.g. Green = model · Blue = you

reward.badge: string | null — non-null only when quest.position = 3
reward.message: string — celebration text shown on lesson complete screen

content_schemas._note: The shape of rounds[].content for each lesson type.

scrambled_story._how_it_works: order_task runs BEFORE model/practice/perform. After correct order → model reads assembled story → practice → perform.

order_task.prompt: string — instruction shown above the drag task
order_task.on_correct: string — message shown when order is right
order_task.on_wrong: string — message shown when order is wrong

pace_difference._how_it_works: Model plays both speeds. Practice = slow only. Perform = both speeds recorded separately, each scored.

emotion_difference._how_it_works: Model plays all emotions. Practice = first emotion only. Perform = one at a time, each scored.
emotion_difference._bucket_rule: Spark = 2 emotions, hints visible in perform. Explorer/Achiever = 4–5 emotions, no hints in perform.

character_read._how_it_works: Model plays both characters. Practice = first character only. Perform = each character recorded separately, each scored.

characters.name: string
characters.voice_hint: string — delivery instruction for this character
characters.color: string — hex color for UI badge

tongue_twister._how_it_works: Model plays all 3 speeds. Practice = slow only. Perform = all 3 speeds recorded separately, stars awarded per clarity threshold.
tongue_twister.focus_sound: string — e.g. S — letter highlighted in UI

speed_reader._how_it_works: Model demos target pace with 3 sample sentences. Practice = 2 sentences, no timer. Perform = timed sprint, score = count × clarity.
speed_reader._bucket_rule: Spark = 20s, 4–5 word sentences. Builder = 25s. Explorer/Achiever = 30s, complex sentences.
sentences: array of 10–15 strings — pool to draw from during timed sprint

listen_and_echo._how_it_works: Model plays sentence (visible). Sentence disappears. Practice = hidden, peek button available. Perform = hidden, no peek, sentence revealed after scoring.

scene_speak._how_it_works: 3 sequential domain steps: step_1 read paragraph (scored), step_2 write one sentence (not scored — scaffold only), step_3 speak description (scored).
scene_speak._note: Writing task in step_2 is a scaffold for speaking in step_3. It is not a writing assessment.
scene_speak.writing_prompt: string — shown in step_2, e.g. In one sentence — what is the most important thing happening here?
scene_speak.speaking_prompt: string — shown in step_3, e.g. Describe what you see in your own words.

comic_reader._how_it_works: Model reads all panels and all characters. Practice = student reads full script once, not scored. Perform = student reads entire comic, both voices, scored on vocal variety.

refresher._how_it_works: No model. No practice. Straight to 3 perform moments pulled from completed lessons. Scoring weight 0.75 (lighter than standard).
refresher._unlock_rule: Only available after student has completed 5+ lessons.
source_lesson_ids: array of completed lesson_ids to pull moments from

moments.type: string — lesson type of the source lesson
moments.label: string — e.g. ⚡ Quick Emotion
moments.instruction: string — short, e.g. Say it ANGRY — 8 seconds!
moments.content: object — exact content pulled from source lesson

topic_read._how_it_works: Student picks a topic they like. Model reads passage. Practice = student reads once. Perform = fluent scored read. Topic is engagement only — not a learning goal.
topic_read._removed_fields: vocab_words and comprehension_question removed — those are content learning, out of scope.

delivery_flashcard._how_it_works: Card front shows phrase. Card flips to back. Back shows delivery instruction — HOW to say it, never WHAT it means. 3 cards per lesson.
delivery_flashcard._mission_check: Card back must never explain vocabulary or meaning. Only delivery: which word to stress, what tone, what volume.

back.target_word: string | null — for stress_card only, the word to emphasise
back.tone_label: string | null — e.g. 😐 Calm and certain
back.scenario: string | null — for voice_card: context for why this volume

voice_signals_reference.pronunciation: Clarity of sounds, consonant precision, vowel accuracy
voice_signals_reference.stress: Word-level emphasis — which words stand out above others
voice_signals_reference.task_adherence: Followed topic, format, and structure of the task

quest.lesson_type: same type for all 3 lessons in this quest

position_difficulty_rule.position_1: most scaffolding, shortest content, hints shown in practice only
position_difficulty_rule.position_2: medium scaffolding, hints in practice only, slightly longer content
position_difficulty_rule.position_3: least scaffolding, no hints in perform, badge awarded on completion

validation_checklist._note: Run these checks before saving any lesson JSON.
checks: meta.lesson_id is unique across all lessons
meta.grade_bucket is one of: spark | builder | explorer | achiever
meta.lesson_type matches rounds[].type for all rounds
every round has model + practice + perform (except refresher)
perform.scoring.signals_measured is a subset of meta.signals
refresher: auto_generated = true and source_lesson_ids has 3+ entries
delivery_flashcard: card.back contains no vocabulary definitions or meanings
